title: HOOK — beach arrival
prompt: Wide cinematic kid-show comedy shot on @beach-shore — bright sunny morning, gentle blue surf rolling in softly, soft golden sand, blue sky with a few cotton clouds. There are EXACTLY 3 people in the entire frame — no extra adults, no extra children, no background figures, no ghost extras. @Mama already CENTER-LEFT on the dry sand standing with feet planted on the sand, both hands holding a striped beach bag at her side, sun hat and sunglasses on, smiling brightly with warm crinkled eyes. Mama has exactly two arms and two hands visible. @Sara already CENTER-RIGHT next to Mama also standing with both feet planted on the sand, holding a yellow plastic bucket in her right hand and a small red shovel in her left hand, ponytail bouncing softly, beaming with excited dad-show energy. Sara has exactly two arms and two hands visible. @Eva already FAR-RIGHT next to Sara also standing with both feet planted on the sand, holding a blue inflatable float ring with both hands at her side, curly blonde hair with rainbow bow, eyes shining with excitement. Eva has exactly two arms and two hands visible. All three stand grounded on the sand looking out at the beach with cheerful warm smiles. Sara (cheerful warm voice, looking at the surf): "BEACH DAY!" Eva (giggling, shaking the float ring softly): "Sandy toes! Salty knees!" Mama (warm laugh): "Let's find a good spot for our blanket." Cheerful warm kid-show family lighting throughout. Soft sun, gentle breeze, calm water. Preserve each character's silhouette and proportions throughout — no morphing of facial features, no shifting of hair or wardrobe, no color drift on clothing or skin tone. Keep all character bodies stable, consistent in scale, and locked to the same outfit and hairstyle from second 0 through the final second. STRICT ANATOMY: each character has exactly two arms, two hands, two legs at all times. Each character stays grounded with both feet in contact with the sand throughout. No leaping, no jumping, no running off-screen. The sand stays plain clean golden sand throughout — no marks, no trash, no other people on the beach.
